titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Agnes Campbell and Kent Kramer Collection of Margaret Anderson
Campbell (Agnes) and Kent Kramer Collection of Margaret Anderson
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 00:27

did.unittitle: Agnes Campbell and Kent Kramer collection of Margaret Anderson

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, personal papers, and printed material by or relating to American writer and editor Margaret Anderson and others.

bioghist.head: Agnes Campbell (1885–1977)
bioghist.p: Agnes Campbell Darrow was Margaret Anderson's roommate at the Western College for Women in Oxford, Ohio. The two remained lifelong friends until Anderson's death. Campbell was born in Dayton, Ohio in 1885 and later studied at the University of Chicago. She married William Darrow, with whom she had two children: Harman Darrow and Dorothy Kramer.

bioghist.head: Kent Kramer (1944?–2019)
bioghist.p: Kent Darrow Kramer was the grandson of Agnes Campbell. He was born in Ohio to Dorothy Kramer and lived in Washington, D.C. where he worked for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection. He was an active member of the Gurdjieff Society of Washington, D.C.

bioghist.head: Margaret Anderson (1886–1973)
bioghist.p: Margaret Anderson was an American writer, editor, and founder of the literary magazine Little Review . Born on November 24, 1886 in Indianapolis, Indiana, Anderson attended the Western College for Women for two years, where she befriended her roommate Agnes Campbell. Anderson moved to Chicago in 1908, working as a book reviewer and editor for various publications before founding the Little Review in 1914. The magazine centered on avant-garde literature, art, and criticism and published the work of influential modern writers such as T. S. Eliot, Ernest Hemingway, and William Carlos Williams.

In 1916 Anderson began a romantic relationship with artist Jane Heap, who subsequently became co-editor of the Little Review . The magazine moved to an office to New York City in 1917 and began serializing James Joyce's novel Ulysses in 1918. Upon publishing the book's "Nausicaa" episode in early 1920, Anderson and Heap were arrested and charged with obscenity. They were found guilty in a New York court, forcing Little Review to cease publication of Ulysses in 1921.

After stepping down as editor of Little Review in 1923 and ending her relationship with Heap, Anderson moved to Paris, where she met French singer Georgette Leblanc. The couple lived together until Leblanc's death in 1941. Anderson also studied under Greco-Armenian philosopher Georges Ivanovitch Gurdjieff in Paris. From 1935 to 1939 she was a member of the Rope, a group of Gurdjieff's female followers that included Heap, Leblanc, Solita Solano, and Kathryn Hulme. Anderson published the memoir The Unknowable Gurdjieff in 1962. Her other books include three autobiographies: My Thirty Years' War (1930), The Fiery Fountain (1951), and The Strange Necessity (1962). Her final book Forbidden Fires was published posthumously in 1996 by editor Mathilda M. Hills. Anderson died on October 19, 1973 in Le Cannet, France.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, personal papers, and printed material by or relating to American writer and editor Margaret Anderson and others. Correspondence consists of personal and professional letters exchanged between Anderson and other writers, editors, publishers, and others such as Agnes Campbell, Elspeth Champcommunal, Emma Goldman, Jane Heap, Kent Kramer, Georgette Leblanc, Solita Solano, and Alice B. Toklas. There is also a small amount of third party correspondence. Writings contain notes and drafts for Anderson's books. Also present are galley proofs for the Little Review , including the first page of the serialization of James Joyce's Ulysses . Other material includes notes, writings, and lectures pertaining to Georges Ivanovitch Gurdjieff, under whom Anderson studied while living in France. Included is Anderson's account of her visit with John G. (John Godolphin) Bennett, another student of Gurdjieff. The collection also contains a small amount of papers by or relating to Dorothy Caruso, Anderson's romantic partner.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Correspondence, 1933–1973. II. Writings, 1917–1973. III. Papers Relating to Georges Ivanovitch Gurdjieff, 1926–1964. IV. Photographs, circa 1900s–1973. V. Personal Papers, undated. VI. Dorothy Caruso Papers, circa 1951–1955. VII. Printed Material, 1923–1973.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Agnes Campbell and Kent Kramer Collection of Margaret Anderson. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Agnes Campbell and Kent Kramer Collection of Margaret Anderson is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Margaret Anderson Correspondence, and Third Party Correspondence. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: Correspondence can also be found in Series III. Papers Relating to Georges Ivanovitch Gurdjieff and Series VI. Dorothy Caruso Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Anderson's correspondence with writers, editors, publishers, colleagues, and friends. Correspondents include Vogue editor Elspeth Champcommunal; singer Georgette Leblanc; writers Emma Goldman, Jane Heap, Solita Solano, and Alice B. Toklas; and friends Agnes Campbell and Kent Kramer. Also present is third party correspondence, including letters to Solano.

did.unittitle: Hecht, Rose (Mrs. Ben) and Jackie Caruso
Janin, J. B. to Monique Serrure
Leblanc, Georgette to [first name unknown] Dood

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Writings of Margaret Anderson Correspondence, and Writings of Others. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by title of work or author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings contains notes and drafts pertaining to Anderson's books and galley proofs for Little Review . Included are the unfinished and unpublished work "Collection," a compendium of letters and writings by Anderson, and the first page of the serialization of James Joyce's Ulysses . The series also contains writings by Lynn Clark and Solita Solano.

did.unittitle: Forbidden Fires [working title: "Love Story"], drafts, typescript
Little Review , Volume 4, Number 1, galley proof [with note to Agnes Campbell]
Little Review , Volume 5, Number 11, galley proof: Ulysses , Episode 1
Papers Relating to Georges Ivanovitch Gurdjieff

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes notes and drafts for Anderson's book The Unknowable Gurdjieff ; notes and transcripts of Jane Heap's lectures and meetings regarding Gurdjieff; letters exchanged between Gurdjieff's students; and writings by others pertaining to Gurdjieff. Also present is Anderson's account of meeting John G. Bennett, an English writer and student of Gurdjieff.

did.unittitle: The Unknowable Gurdjieff , notes and drafts
Anderson, Margaret to Jeanne de Salzmann
Anderson, Margaret to [first name unknown] Dor

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints of Anderson, her friends, and her lovers, including Georgette Leblanc; scrapbook pages assembled by Anderson; and photographs and picture postcards sent to Agnes Campbell and Dorothy Kramer.

did.unittitle: Picture postcards to Agnes Campbell and Dorothy Kramer
Photographs and letter to Agnes Campbell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Anderson's assorted notes and identity cards.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains diaries, letters, and personal papers by or relating to Anderson's lover Dorothy Caruso, an American writer and the widow of opera singer Enrico Caruso. The couple lived together in France from 1942 until Caruso's death in 1955, whereupon Anderson inherited Caruso's papers. In 1973 Anderson transferred some of Caruso's papers to Dorothy Kramer, who mailed them to her mother Agnes Campbell in Ohio.

did.unittitle: Letters to and from Vera Daumal Page

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes clippings pertaining to Anderson, Dorothy Caruso, and Georgette Leblanc, souvenir programs for performances of the Gurdjieff movements, issues of the Little Review inscribed by Agnes Campbell, and other printed material.

did.unittitle: Institut du développement harmonique de l'homme de G. Gurdjieff , Number 1
